rationale: Both notes indicate a close conceptual and methodological link: the 2012 citing paper models tariff choice/usage with Bayesian learning under nonlinear tariffs, and the 2007 cited paper develops a hierarchical Bayesian learning model of plan choice and usage under increasing-block nonlinear pricing in wireless service. Given this strong overlap in domain (wireless tariffs), constructs (usage learning, plan choice), and modeling approach, this is plausibly a substantive foundation/method source rather than an incidental mention.
